result:
The R script has been successfully executed. The output file data/output/cleaned_data.csv has been created with 16,111 rows and 2 columns (NSID, alcfst). The alcfst variable contains the earliest age of alcohol consumption for each cohort member, with the following distribution:

- Age 14: 6,155 individuals
- Age 15: 2,707 individuals
- Age 16: 1,227 individuals
- Age 17: 1,060 individuals
- Age 19: 654 individuals
- Age 20: 137 individuals
- Age 25: 426 individuals
- Age 32: 142 individuals
- Never had alcohol (99): 366 individuals
- Don't know/insufficient information (-8): 3,237 individuals

The script correctly:
1. Loaded all 8 tab-delimited files from data/input/
2. Selected NSID and alcohol-related variables from each wave
3. Applied the special rule for age 14 (requiring both ever had alcohol AND had alcohol in last 12 months)
4. Used AUDIT frequency items for ages 25 and 32 (response > 1 indicates drinking)
5. Derived the earliest age of drinking for each individual
6. Assigned code 99 for confirmed never drinkers and -8 for unknown status
7. Converted alcfst to a factor with appropriate labels
8. Output only NSID and alcfst to data/output/cleaned_data.csv
